other_observers: Andrew Spencer, Joey Kellner, Mark Peterson, Cole Wild, Brandon Percival, Joe and Steve Mammoser;
specific_location: at the eastern base of Hoy Mountain about 100 to 200 hundred feet from the Utah boarder
description: Initial sight of the 1st bird was from the left side of the bird showing the crest on the head. The bird spent several minutes on top of a log about 20 to 30 feet in front of me. The bird turned to the right showing the tail spread out and the distinct dark tail band. The band looked black with brown above it. As the bird turned further to the right the dark feathers in the neck ruff came into view. The over all size and shape of the bird was slender with the tail being well pronounced. The second bird was observed several different times with a partial tail band, the band was observed on the outer tail feathers. The bold dark bars on the flanks of the 2nd bird were very noticeable.Behaviors: The 2 birds were found in a dense Aspen grove. The first bird, assumed to be a male was on a log about 15 to 20 inches in diameter. The second bird when I first saw it was walking on the ground about 40 feet from the first bird.Call: I did not hear the drumming that others in the sighting party heardPlumage: brown in between the gray and rufous
